_comment: Full AlignUI 2.0 template inventory: every Base + Product component, documented from the template Figma file (props/states mirror the real component sets; visuals in assets/components/ are exported from it). The first five entries carry hand-authored variant examples and demo previews; the rest are design-stable but not code-connected — scaffold them from their spec (build-ui flow) and fill in codePath/examples as they ship.

components.name: Button
components.description: Primary action trigger. AlignUI API: three types (primary, neutral, error) crossed with four styles (filled, stroke, lighter, ghost) and four sizes.
components.tagline: The main action on a screen
components.preview: <button class="ds-btn ds-btn--primary">Save changes</button><button class="ds-btn ds-btn--secondary">Cancel</button><button class="ds-btn ds-btn--ghost">Learn more</button>

examples.title: Primary filled
examples.description: The main action on a view. Use exactly one.

examples.title: Neutral stroke
examples.description: A supporting action shown alongside primary.

examples.title: Ghost
examples.description: Low-emphasis action for tertiary or inline use.

examples.title: Sizes
examples.description: Four sizes share one shape: medium 40px, small 36px, x-small 32px, 2x-small 28px.

examples.title: Disabled
examples.description: Non-interactive. Keep the label so intent stays clear.

anatomy: Icon (optional, leading or trailing, or icon-only)

do: Use one primary-filled button per view
Use type="error" only for destructive actions
dont: Stack two primary buttons side by side
Use a button for navigation, use a Link Button

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Single-line text field with label and hint text. Types cover the common input formats; siblings Tag Input, Counter Input, and Digit Input share the same state model.

examples.title: Default
examples.description: Resting field with a visible label.

examples.title: Focus
examples.description: Focus ring uses the primary token, matching buttons and links.

examples.title: Error
examples.description: Error state colors the border and hint text by intent.

examples.title: Disabled
examples.description: Dimmed surface signals the field cannot be edited.

anatomy: Label (with optional required mark, sublabel, info icon)

do: Always pair with a visible label
Show errors as hint text below the field, colored by state/error
dont: Use placeholder text as the only label

components.name: Badge
components.description: Small status or count indicator. Four styles (filled, light, lighter, stroke) across the 10-color palette; Status Badge is the semantic sibling (completed, pending, failed).

examples.title: Filled
examples.description: Strongest emphasis. Use for counts and unread states.

examples.title: Lighter
examples.description: Quiet tinted label for status that should not pull focus.

do: Pick the color by intent (state tokens), not decoration
dont: Put sentences inside a badge

components.name: Tag
components.description: Removable label for categorizing items. Leading content can be an icon, avatar, flag, or logo; dismiss icon is a toggle.

examples.title: Stroke, dismissible
examples.description: Carries a remove control for dismissing the selection.

examples.title: Gray, read-only
examples.description: No remove control. Use for fixed categories.

anatomy: Leading content (optional: icon, avatar, flag, logo)

do: Group tags on one row where possible
dont: Use tags for actions, they read as passive labels

components.name: Widget Box
components.description: Surface that groups related content and actions — the container behind every dashboard widget in the template's Product Components.
components.preview:
Weekly report

Your team shipped 12 changes this week.

examples.title: Raised
examples.description: Default elevation. Use for standalone surfaces on the canvas.
examples.code:
Weekly report

Your team shipped 12 changes this week.
examples.usage:
<WidgetBox>
  <WidgetBox.Header title="Weekly report" />
  <WidgetBox.Body>Your team shipped 12 changes this week.</WidgetBox.Body>
  <Button type="neutral" style="stroke" size="small">View</Button>
</WidgetBox>

examples.title: Flat
examples.description: No shadow. Use when widgets sit inside another surface.
examples.code:
Weekly report

Your team shipped 12 changes this week.

do: Keep one clear primary action per widget
dont: Nest widget boxes more than one level deep

components.name: Compact Button
components.description: Small icon-only button (20/24px) for dense UI: dismiss, expand, inline actions. Optional full-radius.
components.tagline: Tiny icon-only button for dense UI

do: Use inside cards, tags, and list rows where a full Button is too heavy
dont: Use for the primary action of a view

components.name: Link Button
components.description: Text-styled action for navigation and inline actions, with optional underline.
components.tagline: A text link styled as an action

do: Use for navigation and low-emphasis actions
dont: Mix link buttons and ghost buttons for the same emphasis level

components.name: Social Button
components.description: Third-party auth button (Apple, Google, GitHub, X, Facebook, Dropbox, LinkedIn) in filled or stroke style, full or icon-only.
components.tagline: Sign in with a third-party account

do: Keep every social button the same style on one screen
dont: Recolor brand icons — use the official marks

components.name: Fancy Button
components.description: Elevated, gradient-edged button for marketing moments and standout CTAs.

do: Reserve for one hero action per page
dont: Use in dense product UI — use Button

components.name: Button Group
components.description: A row of 2–6 connected buttons acting as one control (view switchers, bulk actions). Segments support text, icons, or both.
components.tagline: Connected buttons acting as one control
anatomy: Button Group Item (per segment: label, optional left/right icon)

do: Keep segment labels one word
dont: Use for mutually exclusive form choices — use Radio or Segmented Control

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Binary control with indeterminate support. Ships in three layers: bare Checkbox, Checkbox Label (label + description, flippable), and Checkbox Card (selectable card with icon/avatar/logo leading content).

do: Pair every checkbox with a clickable label
dont: Use for mutually exclusive options — use Radio

components.name: Radio
components.description: Selects exactly one option. Same three layers as Checkbox: bare Radio, Radio Label, and Radio Card (basic, left icon, avatar, card provider, brand, company).

do: Always show at least two options in a group
dont: Use radios when more than one choice can apply

components.name: Switch
components.description: Immediate on/off toggle. Layers: bare Switch, Switch Label (description, flip), Switch Card, and Integration Switch (brand logo + description row for settings/integration lists).
components.tagline: Toggle a setting on or off

do: Use for settings that apply instantly
dont: Use inside a form that needs a submit step — use Checkbox

components.name: Select
components.description: Dropdown-triggering field. Value can carry an icon, flag, avatar, or logo. Compact Select (borderless trigger), Compact Select for Input (embedded in Text Input), and Inline Select are the sibling variants.
components.tagline: Pick a value from a dropdown

do: Use for 5+ options where radios would be too long
dont: Use for 2–3 options — prefer Radio or Segmented Control

components.name: Text Area
components.description: Multi-line text field with optional label, hint text, and character counter.

do: Give it a sensible default height of 3–4 lines
dont: Use for single-line values — use Text Input

components.name: Tag Input
components.description: Text field that turns entries into dismissible Tags.

do: Show remaining-count limits as hint text
dont: Use when options come from a fixed list — use a multi-select

components.name: Counter Input
components.description: Numeric stepper field with increment/decrement controls.

do: Disable the decrement/increment control at min/max
dont: Use for large free-range numbers — use a Text Input type=amount

components.name: Digit Input
components.description: Fixed-length code entry (OTP / 2FA), one box per digit.
components.tagline: One box per digit for codes (OTP)

do: Auto-advance focus as digits are typed
dont: Use for anything other than short verification codes

components.name: Slider
components.description: Single-value or range slider on a horizontal track, with optional thumb tooltip and label/sublabel.
components.tagline: Drag to set a value or range

do: Show the current value (tooltip or label) while dragging
dont: Use for precise required values — pair with a Counter Input

components.name: Rating
components.description: Star/heart rating plus survey-style rating bars (emoji, number, star, heart scales). Rating & Review adds description and link button layouts.
components.tagline: Star or heart ratings and reviews

do: Support half steps in display mode
dont: Mix scale types in one flow

components.name: Date Picker
components.description: Calendar for single dates or ranges. Composed from Date Selector (month navigation), Day Labels, Day Cells (with marked/disabled/range states), and Period Range presets; footer holds buttons and hint text.
components.tagline: Choose a date or date range

do: Disable unavailable days instead of erroring after selection
dont: Use a picker for well-known typed dates like birthdays — use Text Input type=date

components.name: Time Picker
components.description: Time selection list with optional secondary time (timezone) display, duration selector, and availability status rows. Composed with Select and Scroll parts.
components.tagline: Choose a time from a list

do: Keep the list scrollable with a sensible default position (now, rounded up)
dont: Force second-level precision when minutes are enough

components.name: File Upload
components.description: Dropzone (File Upload Area), per-file progress cards (in progress / success / error) with format icons, and Image Upload for avatar/logo slots.
components.tagline: Drop or pick files to upload
anatomy: Upload cards (file name, size, progress, status)

do: State accepted formats and max size in the dropzone copy
dont: Block the whole form while a file uploads

components.name: Color Picker
components.description: Color selection surface: spectrum area, hue/opacity sliders, hex + opacity inputs, and preset Color Dots in the 10-color palette.
components.tagline: Choose a color visually or by hex

do: Offer the token palette as presets first
dont: Let product UI pick arbitrary colors that bypass the system

components.name: Label & Hint Text
components.description: Shared form furniture: Label (required mark, sublabel, info icon, link button), Hint Text (default / error / disabled), and Password Strength meter (empty / weak / moderate / strong).
components.tagline: Labels and helper text for forms

do: Reuse these parts in every field component so spacing stays identical
dont: Color hint text manually — the state prop does it

components.name: Rich Editor
components.description: Rich-text editing toolbar in four width presets, built from toolbar items (text, dropdown, color, icon) and a 10-color swatch palette.

do: Hide advanced controls behind the Show More toggle
dont: Offer colors outside the token palette

components.name: Breadcrumbs
components.description: Hierarchy trail of 3–5 items with arrow, slash, or dot dividers. Items can be icon-only, text-only, or both.

do: Mark the current page as non-clickable
dont: Use breadcrumbs on flat, single-level sites

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Page cells composed into a Pagination Group (basic, full-radius, or grouped style) with first/last and next/previous toggles plus optional totals info.

do: Disable prev/next at the ends of the range
dont: Use pagination for infinite feeds — use load-more

components.name: Tab Menu
components.description: Horizontal (2–6 tabs) or vertical (2–8, card or list style) tab navigation. Items support icons and count badges.
anatomy: Tab item (label, optional icon, optional badge)

do: Keep tab labels to one or two words
dont: Use tabs for a sequential flow — use Step Indicator

components.name: Segmented Control
components.description: Pill-style switcher of 2–3 options with optional label; items can carry a left icon or be icon-only.
components.tagline: Pill switch between a few options

do: Use for instant view switches (list/grid, monthly/yearly)
dont: Exceed three segments — use Tab Menu

components.name: Step Indicator
components.description: Multi-step progress in horizontal or vertical orientation (3–5 steps), plus a full sidebar variant and compact Stepper Dots.
components.tagline: Show progress through a set of steps

do: Mark completed steps as clickable to go back
dont: Use for fewer than three steps

components.name: Alert
components.description: One component covers Alert, Notification, and Toast: five statuses (error, warning, success, information, feature) × four styles (filled, light, lighter, stroke) × three sizes, with optional description, link buttons, and dismiss.

do: Pick status by intent and let the state tokens color it
dont: Put critical, must-read content in an auto-dismissing toast

components.name: Banner
components.description: Full-width top-of-screen announcement using the same status × style matrix as Alert, with optional description, link button, and dismiss.

do: Show at most one banner at a time
dont: Use for per-field errors — use Hint Text

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: Linear bar (0–100% in 10% steps) with label layouts (top/right, optional bottom caption) and a Circular variant (48–80px, optional center number). Bar color communicates status.
components.tagline: Show how far along a task is

do: Use a determinate bar whenever progress is knowable
dont: Animate to 90% and stall — show real progress

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Hover/focus hint with 8 tail placements and three sizes; Large adds a description, icon, and dismiss. Tail can be hidden; dark and light modes built in.
components.tagline: Small hint shown on hover or focus

do: Keep tooltips to a short phrase
dont: Put essential information only in a tooltip

components.name: Avatar
components.description: User/entity representation in 9 sizes (20–80px) with image, initials, icon, memoji, or illustration content; bottom status (online, offline, busy, away, company) and top status (verified, pin, favorite, add, remove, notification) indicators; Avatar Group and Compact Avatar Group for collections.
components.tagline: Picture or initials for a person

do: Provide a text alternative for the image
dont: Rely on the status dot color alone to convey state

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Semantic sibling of Badge for workflow states: completed, pending, failed, information, disabled — in light or stroke style, with optional dot.

do: Use in tables and lists where states repeat
dont: Invent new status colors — these five map to the state tokens

components.name: Table
components.description: Tables compose from Table Header Cell (with Sorting Icons) and Table Row Cell — no monolithic component. Row cells set text priority (leading, regular, passive) and can embed Button, Button Group, Switch, Rating, Progress Bar, Status Badge, Badge Group, or Avatar Group.

do: Left-align text, right-align numbers, one leading column per row
dont: Embed more than two interactive components per row

components.name: Accordion
components.description: Expandable disclosure row with optional left icon and a flippable chevron position.

do: Allow multiple items open unless space demands otherwise
dont: Hide critical content behind an accordion by default

components.name: Activity Feed
components.description: Chronological event list with typed rows: default, file attachment, comment, avatar group, and task status (success, warning, pending, error), plus filter chips.

do: Group items by day when the feed spans days
dont: Mix relative and absolute timestamps in one feed

components.name: Notification Feed
components.description: Notification panel composed of header, tab menu (2–4 tabs), typed items (basic, button, file, message) separated by content dividers, and a footer with shortcut links.

do: Offer mark-all-read in the header
dont: Auto-dismiss unread notifications

components.name: Content Divider
components.description: Separator in nine flavors: plain line, spaced line, text, text-and-line, solid text, and icon/text button variants (with grouped-button forms) — used inside dropdowns, tables, and modals.

do: Use the text variants as list section headers
dont: Stack dividers without content between them

components.name: Scroll
components.description: Custom scrollbar in two visual weights (default, lighter) and three widths (medium 20, small 16, x-small 12).

do: Use the lighter style over content imagery
dont: Hide scrollbars on long lists entirely

components.name: Modal
components.description: Composed surface: Modal Header (basic, left icon, or status: error/warning/success/information; 56/80px) + free content + Modal Footer (basic, checkbox, toggle, stepper, link button, stretch) over the Modal Overlay scrim. Status Modals are ready-made confirmations.
components.tagline: A dialog that overlays the page
anatomy: Header (title, optional description or status icon, close)

do: Return focus to the trigger on close
dont: Stack modals on top of each other

components.name: Drawer
components.description: Side panel composed from Drawer Header (basic or left icon; small/large) + free content + Drawer Footer (basic, checkbox, toggle, stepper, link button, stretch).
components.tagline: Panel that slides in from the side

do: Use for detail views and multi-field editing that keeps page context
dont: Use a drawer for a blocking confirmation — use a Modal

components.name: Popover
components.description: Anchored floating panel with 12 tail placements (tail hideable) and an optional footer (stretch buttons or stepper) for multi-step hints and tours.
components.tagline: Floating panel anchored to an element

do: Use the stepper footer for product tours
dont: Use a popover for destructive confirmations — use a Modal

components.name: Dropdown
components.description: Menu items for selects, context menus, and command surfaces. Items (small 36 / large 56) carry avatars, flags, or logos plus optional checkbox, sublabel, badge, shortcut, toggle, or button; misc items add search, buttons, and captions. The container is a 16px-radius surface with the md shadow, assembled per use.

do: Keep one interaction pattern per menu (select or command, not both)
dont: Exceed ~8 visible items without search

components.name: Command Menu
components.description: ⌘K palette composed from a search input, typed result items (basic, avatar, left icon, brand, company, country; 48/64px), and a shortcut footer.

do: Order results by recency and match quality
dont: Bury primary navigation exclusively in the command menu

components.name: Sidebar
components.description: App navigation panel with collapsed mode, composed from Sidebar Header, Header Card, Sidebar Items, Feature Cards (six types × four styles), User Profile Card, and Sidebar Footer.

do: Persist the collapsed state per user
dont: Nest navigation more than two levels deep

components.name: Topbar
components.description: Top app bar with page context and actions, composed from Topbar Items, Item Buttons, and User Profile.
components.tagline: Top app bar with page actions

do: Keep global actions (search, notifications) consistent across pages
dont: Duplicate the sidebar's navigation in the topbar

components.name: Page Header
components.description: Page-level (88px) and section-level (80px) headers in five types: basic, avatar, left icon, brand, company — title, description, and action slot.
components.tagline: Title and actions for a page

do: Use one page header per view; section headers below it
dont: Put more than two actions in the header — overflow the rest

components.name: Empty State
components.description: Illustrated placeholder shown when a widget or view has no data: illustration tile, message, and optional action.

do: Say what will appear here and how to create it
dont: Show a bare 'No data' string inside a widget
